steps.title: Look up the southern stoplight loosejaw in the table
steps.content: The question asks at what depth the southern stoplight loosejaw lives. According to the table, this species lives 500–2,000 meters below the ocean surface.

steps.title: Match the depth range to each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘More than 2,000 meters below the surface’: the table gives an upper limit of 2,000 meters, not deeper than that. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘150–400 meters below the surface’: this range does not match the table’s 500–2,000 meters for this species. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘500–2,000 meters below the surface’: this exactly matches the depth range recorded in the table for the southern stoplight loosejaw. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘250–500 meters below the surface’: this range does not match the table’s 500–2,000 meters for this species. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
